steps.title: Find the side length of the equilateral triangle
steps.content:
Perimeter = 876 cm. For an equilateral triangle, all three sides are equal:
$$s = \frac{876}{3} = 292 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Apply the Law of Sines to find the circumradius
steps.content:
For any triangle inscribed in a circle: $\frac{a}{\sin A} = 2R$. For an equilateral triangle with angle $60°$ and side 292 cm:
$$\frac{292}{\sin 60°} = 2R$$
Since $\sin 60° = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$:
$$\frac{292}{\sqrt{3}/2} = 2R \implies \frac{292}{\sqrt{3}} = R$$
Rationalize by multiplying by $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}}$:
$$R = \frac{292\sqrt{3}}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for w using the given form
steps.content:
We are given that the radius is $w\sqrt{3}$ cm. Setting this equal to our result:
$$w\sqrt{3} = \frac{292\sqrt{3}}{3}$$
Divide both sides by $\sqrt{3}$:
$$w = \frac{292}{3}$$
Equivalent forms accepted: 292/3, 97.33.
